title: Hookera terrestris
taxonomy: Hookera terrestris is a species of flowering plant in the family Blechnaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the chain ferns or bug ferns. The genus Hookera is part of the subfamily Blechnoideae. It is a terrestrial fern species, found in New Zealand. As a member of the Blechnaceae family, Hookera terrestris is closely related to other fern species within this family.

intro: Hookera terrestris, also known as Hookera, is a type of fern native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Hookera terrestris include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hookera terrestris is often prepared as a tea or infused in a liquid extract, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to support skin health and wound healing.
